114003.explanation: It is about the quality of the leaders. Whether they are men or women does not matter to us.

114004.explanation: The current municipal council was properly elected to decide on this. Programs often clearly state what they stand for. Additionally, residents can always speak up and approach the council group. However, in exceptional cases, it should be possible.

114007.explanation: The APB disagrees with this statement because it explicitly states that the municipality must ALWAYS allow this. The rural area of Etten-Leur is characteristic and diverse. It offers space for living, entrepreneurship, and recreation. These different functions must be able to coexist harmoniously. Therefore, APB does not want to allow unlimited new functions in the rural area but believes the addition of functions must be well considered.

114008.explanation: We support such initiatives, but not financially. However, we do provide assistance in the form of advice and, for example, permits if needed.

114010.explanation: We want to take responsibility to accommodate some of the asylum seekers.

114013.explanation: On the condition that action can be taken in case of unacceptable nuisance. By the way, this has always been the case.

114015.explanation: This could be a consideration, but in our opinion, it should first be investigated whether there is a need for this. Additionally, it is wise to wait for national legislation on this matter.

114016.explanation: Waste collection is never free. It is always accounted for somewhere. However, we are willing to investigate what effects and benefits this has for our residents.

114018.explanation: We consider this a task for the entrepreneurs themselves. However, we can point them to possible subsidies and/or provide advice.

114020.explanation: Individuals/families with an income of 120% of the social assistance norm can qualify for the 'participation scheme'. People with a social assistance benefit also fall under this. We want to maintain this.

114023.explanation: It is up to the parents to pay this. In certain cases (for example, when a child lives in poverty), a claim can be made to the 'participation scheme'.

114024.explanation: The municipality must certainly help sports clubs but this does not always have to be financial. It can also be through advice, expanding accommodation possibilities, promoting cooperation, or pointing out subsidy opportunities.

114030.explanation: In our opinion, there is sufficient variety in housing construction for every target group.

114031.explanation: Under strict conditions and with a permit, we could do this temporarily to reduce pressure on the housing market.

114032.explanation: We want to realize housing construction in Haansberg over the next 4 years, starting with 1,400 homes in the southern part. Additionally, within the same period and with the relocation of Intratuin in that area, we want to add approximately 300 homes. In the longer term, we also see the need to add housing in the northern part of Haansberg. If we only allow housing construction within urban areas, then we will never ensure that everyone in Etten-Leur can find a suitable home.

114035.explanation: There are currently sufficient arrangements in place to receive help if needed. Tailor-made solutions are the motto.

114036.explanation: The municipality must continuously challenge youth work to improve matters.

114038.explanation: This must be assessed per street or area. On certain roads, a speed limit of 50 km/h should be possible to promote traffic flow.

114039.explanation: Within Etten-Leur, various fixed care providers are used (through tendering). However, we are in favor of preserving tailor-made care where necessary or desired.

114042.explanation: Dog tax must be cost-covering. We support well-maintained dog walking areas, but 'the user pays'.

114043.explanation: The tourist tax in Etten-Leur is currently lower than the average in North Brabant.

114044.explanation: There must be enough space to work. At this moment, that space exists.

114045.explanation: They do not need to have priority, but a quota should be made available for them.

114046.explanation: Disposing of waste is never free. This is included in the fee. We prefer to stick to the current system. Residents should be encouraged to dispose of less waste, which can be achieved, among other things, with a financial incentive.

114047.explanation: The Nobelaer has great social value. However, we must always critically assess their financial management.

114049.explanation: Coffeeshops often cause nuisance. One coffeeshop is manageable.

114050.explanation: We find the national percentage better to maintain a balance between feasibility, social cohesion, and the demand for housing. But also from a financial point of view.

114051.explanation: Provided it can be integrated well from an urban planning perspective.

114053.explanation: Paid parking should not be more expensive than competing centers in the region.

114055.explanation: We strive for a car-reduced center. In other words, part of the center should be car-free during certain time periods and accessible at other times. This is to keep/make the center vibrant.

114003.explanation: The ideal situation would be an equal number of men and women in the executive board; however, the most important condition is a qualitatively good board.

114004.explanation: The possibility for citizen participation is always important in a democracy. For a referendum, it is crucial to provide as much complete and accurate information as possible to everyone so that people can make a well-considered choice. This applies both physically and digitally.

114007.explanation: Where possible, farmers should have the opportunity to open a shop or campsite.

114008.explanation: We support new, smart, and small-scale options to generate energy so we can become an energy-neutral municipality as soon as possible.

114010.explanation: Our Etten-Leur stands for a dignified existence. Asylum reception is still necessary now, and we take our responsibility for that.

114013.explanation: We follow national legislation in this matter.

114015.explanation: We favor 2 centrally organized fireworks shows, both in Etten and on the Leur, with accompanying New Year's celebrations.

114016.explanation: There is less chance of litter if more disposal options are available.

114018.explanation: We expect entrepreneurs to initially ensure good access to their buildings themselves.

114023.explanation: Where the child cannot pay, options must be available through the municipality, such as the current minimum arrangement.

114024.explanation: Sports should be possible for every resident and child in Etten-Leur.

114031.explanation: There is national legislation for this, which Our Etten-Leur supports.

114032.explanation: Housing construction must fit the rural character.

114035.explanation: Our Etten-Leur wants every resident who falls below the 120% threshold (120% of the social assistance benefit) to have access to various provisions so they can live a dignified life.

114036.explanation: There must be sufficient personnel with adequate support to keep youth engaged and involved and to gain better insight into the target group. Currently, some young people fall outside activities and help. Youth workers can empower them.

114038.explanation: Within built-up areas, a speed limit of 30 km/h should be applied if necessary for traffic safety.

114039.explanation: You should have a choice, but it must also be practically feasible. Care providers are scarce, and sometimes the desired provider is not available. Then it should be possible to switch to another care provider.

114043.explanation: Tourist tax should be used for a beautiful and attractive Etten-Leur.

114044.explanation: First, fill vacancies on existing sites, both within Etten-Leur and regionally. Additionally, facilities on current sites must be in order.

114045.explanation: Etten-Leur has a national obligation to house a certain number of status holders/refugees. Our Etten-Leur also finds it important that our residents can live in Etten-Leur. We do not agree with standard priority for refugees.

114046.explanation: Several times free bulky waste collection should be possible, but the basic principle must be 'the polluter pays.'

114047.explanation: We have applicable agreements for that. Our Etten-Leur also considers the Nobelaer essential for Etten-Leur.

114049.explanation: We already have a coffeeshop in Etten-Leur and that is sufficient.

114050.explanation: Etten-Leur has a 30% standard and Ons Etten-Leur finds this adequate.

114051.explanation: With the caveat that this fits within the environment.

114055.explanation: Ons Etten-Leur wants to start by making the center car-restricted, after which further measures can be considered.

114003.explanation: It concerns the quality of the executive board.

114004.explanation: A lot of participation has already been organized.

114007.explanation: Provided that it fits within the environment.

114015.explanation: It does not necessarily have to be a fireworks show; it can also be another form (e.g., a light show).

114016.explanation: But within existing agreements (maximum of 2 times per year half a cubic meter picked up for free).

114020.explanation: There are provisions to partially contribute to the costs of membership of sports clubs.

114023.explanation: This also happens through the subsidy to Nobelaer.

114024.explanation: The association must support itself. In case of occasional financial problems, there is the possibility to support the association.

114030.explanation: Suitable not only for seniors but also for youth.

114031.explanation: Is in accordance with national policy.

114032.explanation: Haansberg and Lage Vaart side are the last two expansion areas within the village boundary.

114035.explanation: The current scheme goes up to 120% of the social assistance norm and that is sufficient.

114038.explanation: With the exception of the through (main) roads.

114039.explanation: Care is provided without distinction of religion, culture, or belief. People always have the possibility to indicate their own care preferences.

114045.explanation: Attention is needed for housing status holders.

114051.explanation: Except for locations suitable for this, such as the center, Haansberg.

114055.explanation: We want to make the center car-restricted.

114003.explanation: The Etten-Leur VVD believes that quality, experience, and suitability must be decisive in the composition of the mayor and aldermen board. Diversity is important and enriches the administration, but a mandatory equal distribution should never be more important.

114004.explanation: We believe it is important that residents are well involved in important decisions. Consultation, participation, and good communication from the municipality are essential. At the same time, we believe that a referendum is not always the right instrument for complex considerations.

114007.explanation: We see opportunities for agricultural entrepreneurs. This can contribute to a vital rural area and extra income for farmers. At the same time, we believe this should not always and unlimitedly be possible. There must be attention for spatial planning, traffic safety, and livability.

114008.explanation: We want to stimulate sustainability. Local energy initiatives increase support, strengthen involvement, and contribute to achieving climate goals. Financial support can help, provided it is targeted and financially responsible.

114010.explanation: We believe that Etten-Leur must take its responsibility within national and regional agreements on asylum reception. Simply and quickly closing an asylum reception center is not realistic. However, attention must be given to support, safety, and livability in the area.

114013.explanation: We believe residents should be able to make their own choices about the use of fireplaces and wood stoves. A full ban is difficult to enforce. We acknowledge that wood burning can cause nuisance and health complaints. Therefore, we believe efforts should focus on awareness.

114015.explanation: Community funds must be spent carefully. Organizing a municipal fireworks show entails considerable costs and does not fit a financially cautious municipality. However, the municipality can facilitate or think along about possibilities in the field of safety.

114016.explanation: The Etten-Leur VVD believes that waste policy must be fair, affordable, and feasible. Free bulky waste collection at home sounds attractive but leads to higher costs for the municipality and ultimately for all residents. This prevents increases in waste taxes.

114018.explanation: The Etten-Leur VVD believes it is important that everyone can participate in our society, including people with disabilities. Accessible shops, hospitality venues, and other buildings contribute to inclusion and increase the reach of entrepreneurs.

114020.explanation: Everyone must have the opportunity to play sports and participate in society, including people with a social assistance benefit. Sports contribute to health, social contacts, and prevention of healthcare costs. Financial support from the municipality can help remove barriers.

114023.explanation: We see the added value of music education for the development of children and support initiatives that make culture accessible. At the same time, we believe not every primary school child must be required or automatically given music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

114024.explanation: Sports clubs are the social glue of our society. They provide meeting opportunities, health, and involvement. When sports clubs face financial difficulties, targeted support from the municipality may be necessary to maintain their social function.

114030.explanation: We find it important that seniors can live independently and comfortably in Etten-Leur for as long as possible. More small rental and owner-occupied homes for seniors provide suitable housing and promote housing market flow, which also frees up family homes.

114031.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for recreational use and not for permanent residence. Allowing permanent residence can lead to undesirable situations, such as pressure on facilities, enforcement problems, and blurring the distinction between living and recreation.

114032.explanation: We want to actively tackle the housing shortage. That means we first look at building within existing village boundaries through redevelopment, infill, and smarter use of space. At the same time, we believe new construction outside the village boundaries should not be excluded by default.

114035.explanation: We want to combat poverty effectively and targeted. Help must reach residents who really need it. Generically raising the income limit can lead to less targeted use of resources and dilution of support.

114036.explanation: Youth work is an important investment in prevention, safety, and social cohesion. Good youth work helps prevent nuisances, supports young people in their development, and contributes to safe and livable neighborhoods.

114038.explanation: We consider traffic safety very important. In many streets, a maximum speed of 30 km/h can contribute to more safety and livability. At the same time, a generic reduction throughout the entire built-up area is not always the best solution. So, faster where it is safe and responsible.

114039.explanation: We consider freedom of choice an important principle in healthcare. When people need care, it must be tailored as well as possible to their personal situation, including faith, culture, or belief. This can contribute to trust, quality of care, and well-being.

114042.explanation: The dog tax is outdated and unfair. Dog owners pay for a facility that is disproportionate to the revenue, while enforcement and administration incur high costs. Moreover, dog owners already contribute to municipal facilities through general funds.

114043.explanation: We want to stimulate tourism and recreation because it contributes to vibrancy, employment, and local spending. A tourism tax that is too high can form a barrier for visitors and entrepreneurs in the recreation and hospitality sector.

114044.explanation: A strong local economy is important, with space for entrepreneurs to grow and settle. At the same time, available space is scarce and we must carefully handle landscape, accessibility, and livability. We agree only if there is demonstrable need.

114045.explanation: It is important that people with a residence permit can integrate properly and build a new life. At the same time, the pressure on the social rental market is high and everyone should have perspective on housing in Etten-Leur. Therefore, build for the right target group.

114046.explanation: Waste policy must remain fair and affordable for all residents. Making the recycling center completely free leads to higher costs for the municipality and ultimately higher waste taxes for everyone.

114047.explanation: De Nobelaer is an important cultural and social facility. This facility contributes to livability and connection in our municipality. At the same time, public money must be handled carefully and we must therefore remain critical of expenses and spending.

114049.explanation: The current situation regarding coffee shops must remain manageable. The opening of new coffee shops can lead to additional pressure on public order, safety, and livability in neighborhoods. We see no need to expand this offer and believe the municipality should be cautious in this.

114050.explanation: Building for all target groups: starters, families, seniors, and people dependent on social housing. However, we find a fixed standard of 40% social housing in new construction too rigid and not suitable for every location or neighborhood. Such a high obligation can make projects financially unfeasible.

114051.explanation: Building upwards is an important means to address the housing shortage, especially in a municipality where space is limited. Tall residential buildings can contribute to additional homes, better flow, and more efficient use of available land. Not every location is suitable for this.

114053.explanation: We want an accessible and attractive center for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. Parking fees that are too high can be a barrier. By keeping paid parking affordable or cheaper, we stimulate shopping visits, hospitality, and events.

114055.explanation: An accessible and lively center is essential. A completely car-free center goes too far and can have negative effects on entrepreneurs, accessibility, and visitors dependent on cars. We support initiatives for parts of the center, especially the Markt. In short: a car-reduced Markt, but not a completely car-free center.

114003.explanation: There must be a good balance between men and women, but quality must be the leading factor.

114004.explanation: That is the role of the municipal council. Citizens also have the right to speak and are taken seriously. Contact between the various parties and citizens could be improved.

114007.explanation: We favor customization. If there are no reasons against it, we have no objection.

114008.explanation: If there are subsidies for that, then certainly. Otherwise, it would be preferable if the costs of generation are covered.

114010.explanation: Asylum reception in Etten-Leur is doing excellently. There is no nuisance, and employers can (in time) make good use of these people.

114013.explanation: Use of wood stoves is not desirable, but if it is prohibited, good alternatives must be available first.

114015.explanation: If this can somewhat accommodate the need to set off fireworks, certainly. However, the fallout of toxins from this remains undesirable.

114016.explanation: Many people have to spend extra money to dispose of bulky waste and therefore dump their bulky waste in various places. We hope this will reduce illegal dumping of waste.

114018.explanation: If entrepreneurs cannot afford it, they should be able to appeal to the municipality.

114020.explanation: Lack of money must not be a reason to not participate in our municipality. This also applies to things like music education, etc.

114023.explanation: Only if the parents cannot pay for it themselves. We consider music lessons an essential part of a child's development.

114024.explanation: We favor customization. Independent if possible. Supportive if necessary.

114030.explanation: There is a high demand for this. This way, homes become available for starters and families. It must be affordable, as that is a major obstacle to moving.

114031.explanation: Building should be based on need. Using holiday homes for this is not desirable. The designation should preferably not be changed, but we do support other forms of housing.

114032.explanation: There is a housing shortage and building must therefore take place. However, the outer area should be spared as much as possible.

114035.explanation: People are central. We see that wages have not risen in line with the costs of basic needs. Therefore, it should be examined whether raising the income limit ensures we reach the right target group of residents who cannot sufficiently meet their basic needs.

114036.explanation: We basically disagree here. Proper assessment must be made of who really needs the support.

114038.explanation: Safety comes first. This is also good for the environment.

114039.explanation: If possible, we have no objection. The municipality should not be obligated to do so.

114042.explanation: The current arrangement is good. Costs are incurred for dog walking areas. But no profit should be made on this.

114043.explanation: It is a source of income for the municipality. We do not think that small amounts deter tourists. Moreover, they also use our public facilities.

114044.explanation: Vacant sites and unused buildings must first be used to the fullest.

114045.explanation: Refugees are part of the priority groups. In emergencies, they must receive priority. We must prevent them from becoming homeless.

114046.explanation: Processing waste costs money. That has to come from somewhere. The polluter pays. So no.

114047.explanation: De Nobelaer fulfills a number of essential functions. That must remain and requires funding. De Nobelaer must do its utmost to require as little subsidy as possible. The municipal council must help oversee this.

114049.explanation: As a municipality, we must help promote healthy choices. There is already one. We do not see the need for a second.

114050.explanation: There is a strong need for social housing.

114051.explanation: Where possible – taking the environment into account – certainly.

114053.explanation: We support the STOMP principle. We therefore prefer that people come to the center on foot or by bike. Modern solutions such as (self-driving) shuttle buses should also be considered.

114055.explanation: As much as possible. This makes Etten-Leur more attractive to tourists and shoppers.

114003.explanation: D66 stands for equality and inclusion. A balanced representation of men and women fits good and modern governance.

114004.explanation: D66 promotes the use of instruments of direct democracy such as referenda.

114007.explanation: To allow farmers to engage in sustainable entrepreneurship, there must be room for innovation. Custom solutions, landscape integration, and nuisance are important factors. This should not be limited to a shop or campsite but also include other opportunities for innovative entrepreneurship.

114008.explanation: D66 wants to give local energy cooperatives space and support so that residents can invest together and benefit from sustainable energy. An energy community ensures local generation and usage and reduces pressure on the electricity grid. This also creates more grid capacity for our entrepreneurs.

114010.explanation: Etten-Leur shows that reception can work well. D66 stands for solidarity and good integration. Our asylum seekers center demonstrates that it can be done without nuisance and with broad support from society.

114013.explanation: D66 chooses clean air and health. Wood burning causes nuisance and health risks and does not fit with a sustainable municipality.

114015.explanation: A central fireworks display is safer, more sustainable, and more enjoyable than many private fireworks. This prevents nuisance from private fireworks.

114016.explanation: D66 focuses on a circular waste policy. Free collection encourages waste production and is less compatible with reuse and separation. People with low incomes must be supported with their waste costs.

114018.explanation: D66 stands for inclusion, but it is up to entrepreneurs themselves to arrange this financially. The municipality can provide advice or point to possible subsidies.

114020.explanation: Sports contribute to prevention and the mental and physical health of our residents. D66 wants financial barriers not to hinder participation. This also applies to culture.

114023.explanation: Culture and education contribute to development and equal opportunities. D66 invests in this.

114024.explanation: Sports clubs are crucial for the mental and physical health of our residents, prevention, and social cohesion and deserve support. The same applies to cultural associations.

114030.explanation: More suitable homes for the elderly promote housing flow and independent living. Mixed living arrangements with young people are also possible, fostering social cohesion and resilience.

114031.explanation: The municipality must temporarily allow this due to the housing shortage but it is not the solution to the housing crisis.

114032.explanation: D66 is not afraid of expansion, such as Haansberg, provided greenery and landscape are respected. We also see the value of densifying within village boundaries.

114035.explanation: More people need help due to rising costs. D66 chooses prevention and social security.

114036.explanation: Our youth work prevents problems and contributes to the mental and physical wellbeing of our youth, prevention, and safety.

114038.explanation: D66 supports 30 km/h as the maximum speed where it improves safety and livability. On main access roads where cyclists and cars are separated, the maximum speed may remain 50 km/h to discourage driving through neighborhoods.

114039.explanation: Good accessibility and high quality are paramount in care. Usually, there is a choice of a suitable care provider, but this is not always possible.

114042.explanation: The user pays. Revenue from the dog tax should be used for public space and to maintain dog parks.

114043.explanation: The current level is acceptable and supports facilities and tourism. We use current revenues to invest in tourism and recreation. Since the sector already faces a VAT increase, raising tourist tax in the coming years is undesirable.

114044.explanation: D66 chooses revitalization and efficient use, not new sites. With the recent development of the Frijado site as an additional business park, we have already created extra space.

114045.explanation: D66 stands for solidarity and balance in the housing market. Rapid integration is important but requires care. In Etten-Leur, we meet national targets for housing status holders.

114046.explanation: Offering waste collection for free encourages extra waste. D66 focuses on circular behavior and waste separation. Additionally, residents can already dispose of a standard amount of waste twice a year for free. People with low incomes should be financially supported in this.

114047.explanation: De Nobelaer is the cultural heart of Etten-Leur and deserves ongoing support. Moreover, De Nobelaer fulfills many social functions that contribute to prevention and social cohesion.

114049.explanation: D66 supports regulation and distribution, not prevention. A second coffeeshop can also relieve pressure on the surroundings of the only coffeeshop.

114050.explanation: D66 adheres to the 30-40-30 distribution, with at least 30% social rental housing. To keep housing flow going, it is also important to build enough affordable owner-occupied and mid-range rental homes.

114051.explanation: Where appropriate, high-rise buildings are needed to tackle the housing shortage. Too many people are waiting for a home: young people, elderly, and starters. Therefore, we must take action and show courage.

114053.explanation: The current rate is appropriate. D66 promotes cycling, walking, and public transport. The rate should remain competitive in the region for our entrepreneurs and therefore not increase significantly.

114055.explanation: A car-free center offers more possibilities for a vibrant Etten-Leur. Fewer cars, more space for pedestrians, cyclists, public transport, and quality of stay.

114003.explanation: Quality and not gender should be the qualification.

114004.explanation: There is insufficient listening at major projects, so it is important to have a voice through referenda.

114007.explanation: Definitely an option when looking for alternative incomes. Should be bound by a maximum, though.

114008.explanation: The market must respond to that.

114010.explanation: As far as Leefbaar Etten-Leur is concerned, the reception of asylum seekers should primarily take place in the region they come from. The current influx of asylum seekers entering the Netherlands is unsustainable and disruptive to our society, but also for the asylum seekers themselves.

114013.explanation: Leefbaar Etten-Leur believes it is not up to the municipality to enforce regulations 'behind the front door.' However, informational campaigns can help raise awareness, but the use of fireplaces, wood stoves, and barbecues remains permitted.

114015.explanation: Organizing a fireworks show is not a municipal task. As far as Leefbaar Etten-Leur is concerned, this can and should be handled by local entrepreneurs, with the municipality acting as a facilitator, for example by granting a (free) permit for a fireworks show.

114016.explanation: If the waste collection fee does not decrease, bulky waste should be accepted for free, especially for residents who cannot bring it themselves. This helps prevent littering around containers.

114018.explanation: Entrepreneurs will have to make their buildings more accessible for people with disabilities at their own expense. Leefbaar Etten-Leur does not see this as a municipal task.

114020.explanation: Etten-Leur already has a 'Participation Scheme.' Leefbaar Etten-Leur believes this scheme should be continued.

114023.explanation: Sports are not free either, so why should music lessons be free? Swimming lessons should be free.

114024.explanation: Sports clubs receive subsidies and their facilities are maintained by the municipality. This can also be seen as financial support.

114030.explanation: Research shows that building senior housing also helps other target groups (such as movers and starters). Besides more small rental and owner-occupied homes for seniors, a flow-through scheme should be established to make it easier for seniors to move on.

114031.explanation: Using holiday homes as permanent residences can help alleviate the housing shortage. Attention must be given to maintaining an acceptable living environment. In Etten-Leur, there are (almost) no holiday homes, so this is currently not applicable.

114032.explanation: Enough is enough. The rural area has already had to give up a lot due to housing and new nature development. A livable rural area must remain for all residents.

114035.explanation: Leefbaar Etten-Leur sees no reason to raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance. If the income limit increases further, the incentive to find work or stay employed disappears.

114036.explanation: Leefbaar Etten-Leur has seen no positive effects of youth work in recent years; nuisance in certain neighborhoods has actually increased significantly. Therefore, youth work budgets can be cut, allowing us to, for example, hire extra enforcement officers.

114038.explanation: A 30 km/h speed limit is appropriate near schools and other facilities; for through traffic, 50 km/h is the minimum. Where the situation requires it, such as on roads without separate bike lanes or near schools or other facilities, 30 km/h is necessary.

114039.explanation: Residents who use care purchased or provided by the municipality have no free choice based on religion, culture, or beliefs. If they want this, they must arrange and pay for it themselves.

114042.explanation: The dog tax generates more revenue for the municipality than is spent on dog policy. Collecting the dog tax is expensive, so it should be abolished.

114043.explanation: Tourist tax and water tourism tax should be abolished. Just like with the dog tax (which Leefbaar Etten-Leur also wants abolished), the cost of collecting these taxes is too high compared to the revenue.

114044.explanation: Leefbaar Etten-Leur believes there are already enough industrial parks in Etten-Leur. The Frijado site was ideally suited for housing development.

114045.explanation: Leefbaar Etten-Leur believes the priority status for refugees with residence permits on social rental housing should be removed. Our own residents sometimes wait for years on the social housing waiting list; that must change!

114046.explanation: To combat illegal dumping of waste, waste disposal at the recycling center should be free for our residents. By employing people on benefits at the recycling center, costs can be drastically reduced.

114047.explanation: They now have the chance to come out of the red. If that does not happen, we must immediately cut their subsidies and allocate no further financial resources.

114049.explanation: Currently, there is one coffeeshop in Etten-Leur. Leefbaar Etten-Leur wants to keep it that way; we see no reason to allow an additional coffeeshop.

114050.explanation: About 27% of all homes in Etten-Leur are social rental homes. Leefbaar Etten-Leur believes this can increase to about 30%, which means more than 600 additional social rental homes. We do not think 40% of all homes should be social rentals; there must be a good balance.

114051.explanation: Higher residential apartment buildings are necessary to solve the housing shortage. Attention must always be paid to integration into the environment and parking.

114053.explanation: Leefbaar Etten-Leur supports free parking in the parking garage. This attracts shoppers and has a positive effect on our entrepreneurs, employment, and the local economy.

114055.explanation: Shops and facilities in the center must remain accessible for suppliers and residents, especially those with disabilities who depend on cars or motor vehicles. Leefbaar Etten-Leur believes making the parking garage free can help reduce car presence in the center.

114003.explanation: We focus on people's qualities. The most suitable person gets the position regardless of gender or orientation. We prioritize true equality rather than unfair diversity quotas.

114004.explanation: A reliable municipality ensures influence and involvement of residents in policies that affect them. A referendum can strengthen trust in the municipality.

114007.explanation: JA21 wants to provide space for entrepreneurs, including farmers, where entrepreneurship is rewarded and the government facilitates as much as possible instead of burdening. This freedom leads to economic growth. JA21 opposes paternalism and endless bureaucracy and supports a breathing economy.

114008.explanation: There is great pressure on the Dutch energy grid due to the growth of wind and solar energy (grid congestion), causing overload by a combination of high feed-in (especially on sunny days/weekends) and rising demand for electricity (electric driving, heat pumps).

114010.explanation: The asylum seekers center (AZC) in Etten-Leur opened in 2025 with an agreement until March 31, 2028, but there is an option to extend for 2 years, possibly until 2030. We oppose expanding the current number of asylum seekers and any extension beyond March 31, 2028.

114013.explanation: People decide their own preferences. JA21 opposes rules that unnecessarily restrict people's freedom.

114015.explanation: A professional fireworks show organized by the municipality from 2026 as an alternative to the nationwide total ban. This can be paid by savings on costs (damage). Last year €23,488 plus preventive measures of €12,190.

114016.explanation: No additional costs for placing the residual waste container out on regular collection days.

114018.explanation: An accessible building means people can independently reach their destination.

114020.explanation: No proliferation of minimum income schemes. Working must pay off.

114023.explanation: Music lessons are available at personal cost. The taxpayer should not be unnecessarily burdened.

114024.explanation: Initially, it is up to associations and entrepreneurs to sustain themselves. We already have subsidy schemes and should not create unfair preferential positions. The current council recently abolished the subsidy for sports clubs with fewer than 350 members. This must be reversed.

114030.explanation: JA21 supports housing initiatives such as 'knarrenhofjes' (senior cohousing). Unfortunately, a previous plan to realize this on the Wipakker site did not proceed. We want an alternative.

114031.explanation: Permanent habitation of holiday homes quickly increases the housing supply without extra construction. It offers a solution for house seekers who currently have no alternative. Clear rules can prevent misuse or nuisance.

114032.explanation: We support the 'street more' approach, allowing expansion on the edges of Etten-Leur. Additionally, we utilize infill locations within existing municipal boundaries where possible.

114035.explanation: Also here, carefully look at the individual situation. If you can work, then work and invest in that.

114036.explanation: There is no need to make additional budget cuts here. However, JA21 is critical of municipal spending and if unnecessary items are found, we will develop faster and realistic plans to use the money better, for example to reduce financial burdens.

114038.explanation: We prioritize addressing traffic unsafe locations but ensure smooth traffic flow by not unnecessarily downgrading roads to 30 km/h.

114039.explanation: JA21 advocates putting the citizen and their wishes and needs at the center of care provision. People decide which care provider they choose themselves.

114042.explanation: However, we will ensure proper maintenance of dog walking areas and timely emptying of waste bins there, including those for dog waste.

114044.explanation: JA21 stands for entrepreneurs and workers. Provided this business park brings significant employment, we see a positive impact for Etten-Leur.

114045.explanation: We commit ourselves to the residents of Etten-Leur and to reducing waiting lists on the housing market. It is unfair to give priority to outsiders.

114046.explanation: The waste policy is unclear, expensive, and inefficient. Waste containers are overflowing, and illegal dumping is increasing.

114047.explanation: De Nobelaer in Etten-Leur has received extra financial support from the municipality in several years. Now in 2025, an additional 460,000 euros has been promised again. JA21 recognizes De Nobelaer as an important cultural part of Etten-Leur but believes the deficit should not be continuously covered.

114050.explanation: We want to get rid of excessive regulations that delay building projects or make them unnecessarily expensive. Therefore, we leave housing construction as much as possible to the market, with minimal mandatory building percentages for different housing categories or restrictive sustainability measures.

114051.explanation: But with preservation of our village character and the old core of De Leur.

114053.explanation: To avoid parking pressure in the center, we want to make parking in the underground garage more attractive by offering 1 hour of free parking.

114055.explanation: We advocate for a car-free Market square, provided it is well coordinated with residents and entrepreneurs-with compensation for any adverse effects.

statements.title: Etten-Leur should stop giving money to De Nobelaer.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
More small rental and owner-occupied homes should be built for the elderly in Etten-Leur.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The use of fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Etten-Leur.

titleParts.text: In Etten-Leur moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The center of Etten-Leur should become car-free.
More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Etten-Leur.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Etten-Leur should have more business parks.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The residents of Etten-Leur should be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Etten-Leur moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
At least 40% of the new homes built in Etten-Leur should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Etten-Leur moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.
